ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Proof Of Receipt
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Noell Kubota for George Petalas (Administrator).

Mr. Kubota stated that the deed transferring the property of the Estate to the Beneficiary was recorded on April 5, 2017, that he filed a copy of it with the status hearing statement on May 24, 2017, that the Beneficiary has a tax identification number and that a receipt of distribution was filed on August 8, 2018. Mr. Kubota also acknowledged that he paid himself out of the estate without a Court order, stated that the Executor asked him to perform some services for the benefit of the Beneficiary which he felt were out of the scope and that about $27,000.00 remains on hand. The Petition to Approve Delay in Distribution of Reserve and the accounting petition are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Delay In Distribution Of Assets And Reserve
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar May-14-2018 Continued To Jul-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Delay In Distribution Of Assets And Reserve
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Noell Kubota for George Petalas.

Mr. Kubota stated that the petition was filed in order for an alternate Beneficiary to get a tax identification number, that the Beneficiary just applied to the Internal Revenue Service for a number and that he was told it will take several weeks to do this. The Petition to Approve Delay in Distribution of Assets and Reserve is continued to May 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to address all issues.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Delay In Distribution Of Assets And Reserve
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Noell K. Kubota for petitioner George Petalas.

Mr. Kubota reported that the executor of the estate resides in Virginia, and the lone beneficiary lives in Greece. He stated that the executor desires to make the distribution to the beneficiary, but that the bebeficiary wants the funds deposited in the United States, and to do that he needs a tax ID number. He said that the tax ID has been applied for, but that it typiclly takes the IRS 2-3 months to respond to this type of application. The Court was concerned about the lack of an accounting. Mr. Kubota responded that the beneficiary wiaived the filing of an accounting prior to the approval of the petition for final distribution. The Court observed that there has been a passage of over one year since the approval of final distribution, and there has been a lot of money coming in to the estate since the distribution was approved. He directed the filing of an accounting for the period from the approval of the final distribution to today. The matter was continued to March 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for hearing on the petition to approve the delay and for status of the filing of the accounting.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

Petition To Approve Delay In Distribution Of Assets And Reserve
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Continued To Feb-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Noell Kubota for George Petalas.

Mr. Kubota stated that the real property has been distributed/transferred, that $250,000.00 is still sitting in the Estate account, that the sole Beneficiary is in Greece and has instructed the Personal Representative to set up an account here, and that he has provided a power of attorney form to the Personal Representative. Mr. Kubota also stated that the Beneficiary is slow in responding, that they got an e-mail from the Beneficiary indicating that he is in the process of obtaining a tax identification number, that the rents are $10,000.00 ($8,000.00 net), that the rents are all going into the Estate account and that the Beneficiary has been getting wire transfers of funds. The Court stated that it would like a declaration from the Personal Representative telling the Court what the situation is, what he has been instructed to do, what is contemplated and how long it will take. The Petition to Approve Delay in Distribution of Assets and Reserve is continued to February 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
